RCamelsPE 1.0.0

Read CAMELS-PE data dictionary

Source: R/dictionary.R
read_dictionary.Rd

Reads the unified CAMELS-PE data dictionary containing metadata, catchment attributes, geospatial layers, and time series variables.

read_dictionary(
  category = NULL,
  variable = NULL,
  file = NULL,
  path = get_camels_path()
)

Arguments

category

Character vector or NULL. Optional category filter. Examples include "metadata", "topographic", "climatic", "hydrological", "landcover", "geologic", "soil", "human_intervention", "timeseries", and "geospatial".

variable

Character vector or NULL. Optional variable names used to filter the dictionary.

file

Character vector or NULL. Optional source file names used to filter the dictionary.

path

Character string. Optional path to the CAMELS-PE root directory. If not provided, the path previously defined with set_camels_path() is used.

Value

A tibble containing the CAMELS-PE data dictionary.

Details

The dictionary provides information about variable names, descriptions, units, categories, source files, and data sources available in the CAMELS-PE dataset.

Use this function to explore available variables before calling functions such as read_attributes() or read_timeseries().

The dictionary file is expected at:

01_metadata/data_dictionary.csv

The returned table includes:

  • folder: dataset folder location.

  • file: source file name.

  • category: thematic category.

  • variable: variable name.

  • description: variable description.

  • unit: variable units.

  • source: original data source.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
set_camels_path("path/to/CAMELS-PE")

# Read complete dictionary
dict <- read_dictionary()

# Inspect topographic variables
topo_dict <- read_dictionary(category = "topographic")

# Search for a specific variable
flow_dict <- read_dictionary(variable = "flow_obs")

# Inspect variables from a specific file
stations_dict <- read_dictionary(file = "stations.csv")
} # }
